column1: MCB Manager User Role changes
column28:
A request in a normal flow is still a Manager 1, this manager is the only one that can make Modifications and extensions to one of its Completed requests

 

The requirement is that ANOTHER "MANAGER 2" can make its Modifications and Extensions to requests of "MANAGER 1".

 

It should be clarified that the Completed application of Manager 1 has a documentation code and every time a Modification, Extensions or Corrections is made, the Reference number and Certificate Number are updated based on the First. (they are consequential numbers regardless of MANAGER 1 or MANAGER 2)

 

The restrictions are.

 

Manager 2 should not have access to the information in the original application from Manager 1
Manager 1 should not have access to information from new GESTORA 2 applications

 

LONG-TERM SOLUTION
The solution is to modify backend code, add a column in the database, force a sharing from the database and restrictions for basic functions.

 



Perhaps in the furturo a module to share completed requests between managers. (only if necessary)

column1: Update Project PATH Bar
column28:
Update the logic of the project PATH bar to standardize its behavior for both new and existing projects.

!image-20260316-214821.png|width=484,alt="image-20260316-214821.png"!

----

h3. 1. New Projects

Update the PATH bar behavior as follows:

* The PATH bar must display the {{Name}} of the *Milestones* where the field {{Show_Website__c = true}}.
* The PATH items must be displayed *without numbering*.
* The {{Record Type}} field will no longer be used in this logic.
* The *status of each step in the PATH* must update automatically when the corresponding Milestone has {{Completed = true}}.

----

h3. 2. Existing Projects

A data update must be performed on the Milestones of all existing projects:

* Set {{Show_Website__c = true}} *only if* the {{Milestone Level}} field has a value.
* This update must *not* be applied to:
** *New templates*
** *Projects created using the new templates*

!image-20260316-214900.png|width=484,alt="image-20260316-214900.png"!

* The PATH bar must display the {{Milestone Level}} of the Milestones where {{Show_Website__c = true}}.
* The {{Record Type}} field will no longer be used in this logic.
* The *status of each step in the PATH* must update automatically when the Milestone has {{Completed = true}}.

Enhance Finance quotes in Salesforce to allow defining and persisting *language, currency, and exchange rate*, ensuring that the quote line display and PDF generation correctly reflect this configuration.

!image-20260219-220622.png|width=264,alt="image-20260219-220622.png"!

ORBIS Case Development:
[OCD-0075 | ORBIS Cases-Development | Salesforce|https://orbiscompliance.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/ORBIS_Cases_Development__c/a3NRQ00000N8xH22AJ/view]

* Create a new field to persist the selected *Language and Currency Type* in the quote:
** US Dollars
** Mexican Pesos
** Brazilian Reais

* The quote line view must dynamically update according to the selected currency and exchange rate.
* When generating the quote PDF, the defined language must be automatically set as the document’s default language.
* Once the Opportunity status changes to *“Closed Won”* or *“Closed Lost”*:
** Language, currency, and exchange rate fields must no longer be editable.
** Requote actions must not be allowed.
* Add Grant Total and Discounts, similary to the PDF

11/Feb/26 11:56 AM;712020:8bed8d8d-8a55-42b7-b3c8-8062e9fcedef;Once the manager was created, it wasn't possible to register employees because Salesforce wasn't creating the administrator account. This was due to a code error when sending employee data to the Salesforce API.
The API required the data in the following format.

{noformat}{
  "razon_social":"...",
  "domicilio":"...",
  "rfc":"...",
  "client_id":"...",
  "type":"Agent",
  "empleados": [
    {
      "email":"...",
      "password":"...",
      "nombre_completo":"...",
      "telefono":"...",
      "movil":"...",
      "rol":"Administrador"
    }
  ]
}{noformat}

But the data sent was in the following format

{noformat}{
  "razon_social":"...",
  "domicilio":"...",
  "rfc":"...",
  "client_id":"...",
  "type":"Agent",
  "empleados": {
    "email":"...",
    "password":"...",
    "nombre_completo":"...",
    "telefono":"...",
    "movil":"...",
    "rol":"Administrador"
  }
}{noformat}

This was corrected in the following merge request
[Merge Request|https://devops.orbiscompliance.com/urus/mcb_backend/-/merge_requests/48]
Once corrected, it was possible to update the manager

column1: Production Deploy
Email notification to POC and Account Manager for Engineering Quotation events
column28:
Implement automatic email notifications to the *POC* and the *Account Manager* of the account when the following events occur on an engineering quotation:

* Engineering quotation is completed
* Engineering quotation is sent to Finance
* Engineering quotation is viewed by Engineering

The goal is to keep key account stakeholders informed about relevant actions and status changes related to the quotation.

*Events that trigger the notification:*

# Engineering quotation completed
# Engineering quotation sent to Finance
# Engineering quotation viewed in Engineering

*Recipients:*

* Account POC
* Account Manager

*Required email content:*
The email must include the following quotation information:

* OIS quotation number
* Quotation name
* Account
* Contact
* Quotation status
* URL to the engineering quotation in Salesforce

column1: OT - [7] Calculation and Refactoring of the Project Calendar
column28:
*User observation*

The project schedule is not being calculated correctly up to the Kickoff.

*Technical Description*

Currently, the calendar does not accurately reflect:

* *“Delay”* field in Project Kickoff (Elvys).
* The *Expected Date* , which should be calculated as 4 weeks from the project's Start Date (Ruben).

The generation of the calendar and related alerts in the Smart Table needs to be refactored to ensure the calculations are correct.

*AC:*

* Refactor the calendar calculation logic up to Kickoff.
* Correct the display of the *“Delay” field* in Project Kickoff (Elvys).
* *Expected Date* calculation : 
Expected Date = Start Date + 4 weeks (Ruben).
* Review and correct the generation of alerts in the Smart Table according to the calculated dates.
* Test different date scenarios to ensure consistency and accuracy.

* All samples are registered.

This ensures that no testing project is started without meeting the minimum requirements.

*AC:*

* Implement validation that verifies the approval of all required documents before Kickoff.
* Implement validation to verify that all samples are registered before Kickoff.

Display clear messages to the user indicating what elements are missing

* Review the behavior of the Bridge APIs when the Account does not exist on the website.
* Correct the certificate upload flow according to the technical definition.
* Validate the solution with the provided test case.

column1: OT - [3] Approval of Required Documents without Uploading Files
column28:
*User observation*

According to Javier, many clients will not upload the documents required by the website due to its privacy policies.

*Technical Description*

The system must allow required documents to be *approved, reviewed, or rejected directly from Salesforce*, without requiring the file to be uploaded to Salesforce or the client to send it through the website.

{color:#bf2600}*_Importat note:_*{color}{color:#bf2600} _Javier and Karen indicate that, for internal security reasons, clients will not be sending their files through the website. Even if the client intends to use the website, they will not be sending their files through it due to security concerns, as these are confidential files_{color}_._

*AC:*

* Update API: [https://staging.orbiscompliance.com/api/reviewer/documents/upload|https://staging.orbiscompliance.com/api/reviewer/documents/upload]

If the API is run from Salesforce, the "File" field should not be required.

 

This change will allow us to make Approvals, observations, rejections, *COMMENTS* and *Add NEW DOCUMENTS* to the project, without needing to upload a specific file.

!image-20260204-174821.png|width=264,alt="image-20260204-174821.png"!

* _Another option to resolve this issue without modifying the API would be: we can send an example PDF file internally from Salesforce. In the file, we would write a note indicating the document's status (document approved, document reviewed, document rejected, etc.)_

* Confirm with Karen if EMAIL will continue to be the official channel for tracking required documents.

column1: BUG – Incorrect Status Change in Opportunities
column28:
h4. *Problem Description*

In rare cases, when an Opportunity is moved from the *“Closed Won”* status back to a previous status and then changed again to *“Closed Won”*, the system automatically updates the status to *“Closed Lost”* without user action.

This incorrect behavior is causing significant issues and inconsistencies in Finance reports.

h4. *Reference Opportunity*

Production:

[https://orbiscompliance.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Opportunity/006RQ00000VtIYlYAN/view|https://orbiscompliance.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Opportunity/006RQ00000VtIYlYAN/view|smart-card]

The following Opportunity in DEV24 contains the described issue:
[https://orbiscompliance--dev24.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Opportunity/006Ru00000O2sUkIAJ/view|https://orbiscompliance--dev24.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Opportunity/006Ru00000O2sUkIAJ/view|smart-link]

h2. Test Flow

Test Flow – Programs & Projects

*Applies to easy, difficult, and consulting countries*

----

h2. 1. Opportunity → Program

# Set *"Sent to Client"* on the Opportunity.
# Perform *Close Won*.
# Validate:
#* Automatic creation of the *Program*.
# Perform:
#* *Owner assignment & Notification* on the Program.

----

h2. 2. Program Setup

# Select *Templates* for each *quotation line*.
# Manually create *Projects*.
# Validate:
#* Display of *statistics* in the Program.
#* Automatic creation of *Start of Project*.

----

h2. 3. Documentation Required Alerts

# Validate alert when:
#* The project exceeds *4 weeks from creation* without Documentation Required.
# Display:
#* Alert at the *Project* level.
#* Alert at the *Program* level.

----

h2. 4. Document Management

# Register *Required Documents* in the project.
# Add *comments*.
# Review documents:
#* Approve  
#* Observe  
#* Reject
# Register *new documents*.
# Validate:
#* Documents displayed in the *Smart Table*.
#* Documents displayed in the *Program*.

h3. Special Rule – Documentary Projects

# For *Documentary-type projects*:  
#* When all documents are approved or rejected →  
#*Initial Kickoff is automatically triggered*.

----

h2. 5. Samples Required

# Review the *Samples Required* section.
# Validate the samples list.
# Register *new samples*.
# Register *Arrival Field Sample Date*.

----

h2. 6. Special Rule – Testing Projects

# For *Testing-type projects*:
#* Documents approved/rejected *AND*
#* All sample arrival dates registered  
→ *Kickoff is automatically triggered*.

----

h2. 7. Kickoff & Milestones

# Register *Kickoff* (automatic).
# Validate:
#* Calendar generated in *Milestones*.
#* Dates displayed in the *Smart Table*.
# Register *Submission Date*.
# Validate:
#* Milestone dates are automatically updated.
#* Statuses displayed in the *Smart Table*.

----

h2. 8. Statuses & Variations

# Modify dates to validate:
#* Pending  
#* Complete  
#* Delay
# Validate:
#* Project color coding in the *Program* when there is *Delay*.

----

h2. 9. Certification & Closure

# Upload *certificates* to the projects.
# Validate:
#* Display of uploaded files.
# Open the *public URL* in a new browser.
# Change status to *Certificate Received*.
# Register *Actual Lead Time*.
# Validate:
#* Project color coding in the *Program* when the project is *Completed*.

column1: DEFECT – Error in Consulting Retainer Templates Assignment
column28:
Currently, program items *are not correctly recognizing Consulting Retainer templates*, resulting in an incorrect template assignment during the process.

!image-20260120-133032.png|width=468,alt="image-20260120-133032.png"!

Consulting Retainer templates have the field *“Certification Type” set to “Orbis Services”*; however, this condition is not being considered in the current search logic.

!image-20260120-141259.png|width=468,alt="image-20260120-141259.png"!

*Reference:*
[https://orbiscompliance--dev24.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/MPM4_BASE__Milestone1_Program__c/a0fRu000008ksNNIAY/view|https://orbiscompliance--dev24.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/MPM4_BASE__Milestone1_Program__c/a0fRu000008ksNNIAY/view]

----

h3. *Acceptance Criteria (AC)*

# During the template search, the system *must first filter by the “Certification Type” field*.
# After filtering by “Certification Type”, the system must search for the corresponding template:
#* by *Country*, or
#* by *ISO Code*.
# If the country is *multiple*, the system must automatically assign the template *“MULCON02”*.
# If the country is *single/specific*, the system must automatically assign the template *“SONCON01”*.
# Hide the “Samples” tab and list from Consulting Retainer projects or those with certified Type = “Orbis Services.”

!image-20260120-181546.png|width=518,alt="image-20260120-181546.png"!

column1: Defect: Wrong calculation of the discounts for the Brazil quotes
column28:
*Description:*

The calculus of the Account Special Discount and Complementary Discount for a Brazil quote that has IVA is performed with incorrect values, because the calculus only performs the Exchange Rate from $ USD to R$ BRL using the factor of 5.10, and the value obtained does not reflect the addition of the IVA. For Example:

Actual Calculus Account Special Discount: 

* Account Special Discount (Brazil) = Account Special Discount $ USD x Exchange Rate $ BRL (5.10)

The Calculus should be: 

*  Account Special Discount (Brazil) = Account Special Discount $ USD x Exchange Rate $ BRL (5.10) + IVA (3%) 

Actual calculus Complementary Discount: 

* Complementary Discount (Brazil) = Complementary Discount $ USD x Exchange Rate $ BRL (5.10)

The Calculus should be: 

*  Complementary Discount (Brazil) = Complementary Discount $ USD x Exchange Rate $ BRL (5.10) + IVA (3%) 

*Requirements:*

* Valid Credentials
* Quote with PDF generated

*Steps:*

# Log in to the application
# Go to the QUote
# Click on the OIS Create PDF button.
# Check the values of the Discounts
# Customize the PDF for Brazil ($ BRL)
# Check the values of the discounts

*Attachment:*



*Actual Result:*

The values for the discounts have an error in the calculation

*Expected Result:*

The values for the discounts should be accurate

column1: Invalid JSON Format in Technologies Field
column28:
An issue has been identified with the JSON format stored in the *“Technologies”* field of the *Device Model* object. The value is being received with *unescaped line breaks*, resulting in an *invalid JSON structure*.

[https://orbiscompliance.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Device_Model__c/a3MRQ0000052eZZ2AY/view|https://orbiscompliance.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Device_Model__c/a3MRQ0000052eZZ2AY/view]

!image-20260109-125726.png|width=264,alt="image-20260109-125726.png"!

h4. *Problem Example*

The value is currently being stored as follows:

{noformat}{
  "Safety": "Electric: Has External Power Supply",
  "Telecom": "Bluetooth: Bluetooth 4.0 (BLE); Low Energy (BLE); 2.4GHz 2402-2480MHz
  Radio: UHF 400–470 MHz, UHF 2400 MHz"
}
{noformat}

The *“Telecom”* field contains line breaks that are not properly escaped, which makes the JSON invalid.


It is worth noting that the other technology entries are being processed correctly.

*Impact*

This issue prevents *quotation lines from being displayed*, causing downstream errors in *Finance processes* and during *PDF generation*.

!image-20260109-125902.png|width=264,alt="image-20260109-125902.png"!

*Acceptance Criteria (AC)*

* Identify why the JSON is being received with unescaped line breaks in the *“Technologies”* field of the Device Model.
* Ensure that the value stored in *“Technologies”* is always a *valid JSON*.
* Verify that *quotation lines are displayed correctly*, regardless of the JSON content.
* If the issue is strictly related to JSON formatting, the error should *only impact PDF generation*, not quotation line visualization.
* Implement proper *exception handling* to control formatting errors and prevent financial process failures.

column109:
12/Jan/26 11:07 AM;712020:56472b32-05f9-4cf4-8890-dfc424324468;h3. 📌 *Analysis Report – Salesforce Requirement*

h4. *Requirement Description*

The current requirement requests a modification to the creation of *Certification Detail* within a *Project*, so that in the creation form the *Country* field is *preselected* with the country already associated with the project.

----

h3. ⚠️ *Identified Limitations*

* The *Certification Detail* creation form is a *standard Salesforce component*.
* Standard components *do not allow*:
** Dynamic preselection of field values.
** Additional logic or calculations to populate the *Country* field based on the Project data.

----

h3. 🔍 *Analyzed Options*

The following alternatives were evaluated to meet the requirement:

# *Salesforce Flow*
# *Custom LWC Component*
#* Replicating the *creation* form
#* Replicating the *edit* form
#* Replicating the *list view* with custom buttons

h4. *Impact of These Options*

* They require *multiple structural changes*.
* They significantly increase *development time*.
* They do not allow the use of *triggers* to address the issue.
* They do not fully solve the underlying problem (described below).

----

h3. 🚨 *Identified Technical Issue*

* The *Project Country* comes from a *related object*.
* The *Country* field in *Certification Detail* is a *Global Picklist*, used across multiple functionalities.
* This implementation has existed for a long time, dating back to when projects were *multi-country*.

h4. *Main Conflict*

* If a *Country name* from the Project does *not exactly match* a value in the *Global Picklist*, *preselection would not be possible*.
* This would result in:
** Falling back to the *base case*, where the user must manually select the country.
** Reintroducing the same issue the requirement is attempting to resolve.

----

h3. 🗣️ *Conclusion and Decision*

After reviewing the proposed solutions and their technical impact, and following a discussion with *Saúl*, the following decision was made:

* ✅ *Keep the current behavior*:
** When creating a *Certification Detail*, the user will continue to *manually select the Country*.
* 🔄 This will be *addressed in the future* as part of upcoming changes, where the country structure inconsistency will be corrected.

----

h3. ✅ *Final Status*

* The requirement *will not be implemented at this time*.
* This analysis and decision are documented for future reference.

column1: Review Salesforce Logs in Production
column28:
When an error occurs in the Salesforce production environment, the system sends an email with the error details. As the Salesforce team, it is important for us to have access to these emails to efficiently address the issues.

AC: 


* Coordinate with Gilmar to identify the subject line of the email that Salesforce sends when an error occurs.
* Forward these error emails to Fernando, with a copy to Saul and Kelly.
* Create an Orbis Case to request Gilmar to perform the necessary tasks related to the error.
column109:
07/Jan/26 10:43 AM;5f2ddfb0c1b9f4001cdc43c3;We haven't been able to find a solution or useful help. Below, I describe what was done, studied, and proposed:

* With Gilmar, we define the sender ([info@salesforce.com|mailto:info@salesforce.com]) and a standard subject (script exception), along with a list of recipients to forward emails that Salesforce might generate. Ravi created rules for this action, and we (Ruben, Elvys and me) tested forced errors in both the sandbox and production environments. It didn't work. We don’t receipt any email.
* An alternative was setup the Apex Exception Email.
* I proceeded to study the *possible* reasons why the objective wasn't achieved. Among them were:
** The rules created by Ravi may need refinement.
** Salesforce doesn't send emails for all generated errors.
** In many cases, it sends an email to the last person who modified (LastModifiedBy) a class in production. In our case, it's Vanessa Beltrame's account because we don't have any developer accounts with code deployment and configuration permissions.
** Also this comment found on Stack Exchange: {color:#97a0af}_The Send Apex Warning Emails settings on the User record only control whether or not you receive Apex warning emails every time an Apex exceeds more than 50% of the allocated governor limit. This is available in DE, EE, and UE Editions only._{color}
* Suggestion: Since we can't spend much time researching or testing solutions, we suggest an action that was already implemented in the past: having a computer in Morgan Hill or another trusted location for management, where we can access to the [info@orbiscompliance.com|mailto:info@orbiscompliance.com] account (Salesforce) upon request or through a "request protocol" to obtain such access to debug errors.

Notes:

# *Fixed Price:* For reference only and will not be used in any calculation. In this case, a fixed price does not make sense, since the value depends on the number of CBs or LABs being quoted.

#* Current formula:
_Profit Margin $ = USA Master Price – Break Even (USA Overhead)_

_Profit Margin $ = USA Master Price – Min Price (20% MU)_
# b. *Markup (%)*
#* Current formula:
_Markup % = Profit Margin $ / Break Even (USA Overhead)_
#* It is recommended to change the formula to:
_Markup % = Profit Margin $ / Min Price (20% MU)_

column1: Refactor Program Creation
column28:
Optimize the program creation logic to avoid duplicating quote items when the opportunity status changes.

h3. AC:

* If an opportunity goes through a *Change Request* and then returns to *Close Won*:
** *Quote items should not be recreated in the Program*.
** Items should only be created *the first time*.
* Changes in project scope will be handled manually by the IT team using project statuses (Cancel, On Hold, etc.).
* If a Program associated with an opportunity already exists:
** *Do not create a new Program*.

column1: Refactor Project Creation
column28:
Refactor the project creation logic to standardize the project name, project type, and certification details, ensuring consistency with information from opportunities, templates, and quotes.

h3. AC:

h4. 1. Project Name

When creating a project, the name should follow the format:

*Opportunity Name – Country – Year of Project Creation*

Considerations:

* From the opportunity name, extract only:
** The client name
** The associated model(s)

h4. 2. Project Type

The project type should be determined based on the *“Certification Process”* field of the Template and/or the information in the quote, following these rules:

* *Certification*
** If the Certification Process is *“Document Revision”*
* *Testing*
** If the Certification Process is *“Testing”*
* *Consulting*
** If the quote type is *“Consulting”*
* *Others*
** Should not be considered for project creation

h4. 3. Certification Detail

* In the templates, the *Certification Details* field may contain compound values (e.g. _“Telecom & Safety”_).
* When searching for the template, a *LIKE* query should be used on the Certification Details field.
* If the template’s Certification Detail contains *“Telecom & Safety”*:
** In the project, only the value *“Telecom”* should be registered, based on the corresponding quote line.

column1: Incorrect Naming for Quotes and Opportunities
column28:
*Issue Description:*
Lic. Elizabeth reported that some Finance Quotes and Opportunities are being named incorrectly.

*Example:*

* Correct name in the Engineering Quote (OIS and Salesforce):
*Quote Name:* _iRobot, ESP32-WROOM-32, ESP32-WROCT-33 Multi-Country_
* Name generated in the Finance Quote and the Opportunity:
*Quote Name:* _iRobot, Multimodel, Multi-Country_

This behavior is caused by the *80-character limit* of the Salesforce _Name_ field.
As a result, the quote shown to the customer is incorrect, and Elizabeth must manually rename the records in Salesforce—creating unnecessary work.

For the trazability:
[https://wikiapp.orbiscompliance.com/quotation-test/quotation/previewQuote/U2FsdGVkX1___YnKVui3M71MsVbZzHPJuFgQt%2BEGk0RaM%3D|https://wikiapp.orbiscompliance.com/quotation-test/quotation/previewQuote/U2FsdGVkX1___YnKVui3M71MsVbZzHPJuFgQt%2BEGk0RaM%3D]

column1: Upgrade Nextjs CVE-2025-66478
column28:
The critical security vulnerabilities in Next.js, tracked as *CVE-2025-66478* (and the underlying React issue *CVE-2025-55182*), require an immediate update to specific patched versions to mitigate a Remote Code Execution (RCE) risk. 

Patched Versions for CVE-2025-66478 

You are affected if you use an unpatched version of Next.js 15.x or 16.x with the App Router. The fix is resolved in the following patched releases: 

* *15.0.5*

column1: Update the start and end dates of the Milestones
column28:
*Kickoff* , *Submission* , and *Certificate* dates *Received* at the corresponding milestones , according to the project information. These dates must be updated using specific _triggers_ for each field.

[Tareas Smart Table.docx|https://orbiscompliancellc-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/g/personal/smamani_orbiscompliance_com/IQD7mup2ZruRRKt0tbD2GuiUAVXss6bnc9z4w1PAawa0cHs?e=nUpmew]

!image-20251203-230336.png|width=214,alt="image-20251203-230336.png"!

*Milestones and Update Description:*

·         *Milestone: Project Kickoff*

o    *Kickoff* date is recorded in the project, the "Project Kickoff " milestone should be automatically updated with the following dates:

§  *Start Date (Manual)* : We will coordinate with *Gilmar* to define when this date should be recorded.

§  *End Date (Manual) : This will be automatically set with the* Kickoff registration date in the project.

·         *Milestone: Application Submission*

o    *Submission* date is recorded in the project, the milestone " Application " should be automatically updated. Submission " with the following dates:

§  *Start Date (Manual)* : We will coordinate with *Gilmar* to define when this date should be recorded.

§  *End Date (Manual) : This will be automatically set to the date the* Submission was registered in the project.

·         *Milestone: Certificate*

o    *Actually Time* is recorded in the project, the milestone " Certificate " should be automatically updated with the following dates:

§  *Start Date (Manual)* : We will coordinate with *Gilmar* to define when this date should be recorded.

§  *End Date (Manual)* : It will be automatically set with the date of registration of the Actually Time in the project.

·         *Updating fields and completing milestones :*

o    When a date is entered in the *End Date (Manual) field* of any milestone , a _trigger_ should be launched that updates the *Complete* field to true for that milestone.

For all other milestones , the updating of dates and the *Complete field* will be done as it has been done historically, marking *Complete = true* when the milestone has been completed

column1: Record start and end dates in Milestones — Documentary Projects
column28:
_Kickoff_ date in a document-type project, a _trigger must be executed_ that automatically calculates and updates the *Start Date (Auto)* and *End Date (Auto) columns* of the *Milestones table* , using the values defined in the *TIME column* .

[Tareas Smart Table.docx|https://orbiscompliancellc-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/g/personal/smamani_orbiscompliance_com/IQD7mup2ZruRRKt0tbD2GuiUAVXss6bnc9z4w1PAawa0cHs?e=nUpmew]

*Acceptance Criteria :​*

·         _trigger_ must be developed that calculates and records the start and end dates of each milestone based on the value of the *TIME column* .

·         The calculation should only consider working days (Monday to Friday).

·         After registering the Kickoff , the system should display a confirmation message.

*·*         The message to display should be: *“The project schedule has been established correctly.”*

column1: Quote tool - electrical safety cert issue
column28:
{quote}Artificial intelligence automatically generates electrical safety certification items in countries like Chile and Argentina; however, these are not usually applicable. These countries have different rules: In Chile, there is a comprehensive list of products requiring certification (it's a broad list from the SEC). In Argentina, there is also a product list, but the products must also fall within the following scope: Electrical equipment used with a nominal input voltage for consuming equipment and/or output voltage for generating equipment between 50 V and 1,000 V AC, and between 75 V and 1,500 V DC.

This is just one example; each country has its own rules and criteria regarding the need for local electrical safety certification.{quote}

*GET* /accounts/*:account_id*/contacts/*:contact_id*/*programs*

{noformat}{
  "page_size": 10,
  "page_number": 1
}{noformat}

*Response:*

{noformat}[
  {
    "id": "xxxxxxxxxxx",
    "program_name": "xxxxxxxxxx",
    "created_by": "11/7/2025",
    "oc_owner": "Karen Beltran",       // PMM of the program
    "quote_key": "xxxxx"       // Website quote ID (or null)
  }
]{noformat}

* Document the API in *Confluence*.

column28:
IT is creating a new email and inbox implementation that will improve the workflow for ORBIS staff.
From Orbis case 1765:

{quote}Subject

Create a trigger that, after receiving an email in Salesforce, checks if the Subject has a project number, and copies the email to the project in Salesforce.

 

Description

Create a trigger that, after receiving an email in Salesforce, checks if the Subject line contains a project number and copies the email to the corresponding project in Salesforce.

Currently, Inbox is being used in a testing phase to send emails from Outlook. With this integration, the email is automatically sent to Salesforce. The requirement is that the Salesforce email be checked to see if the Subject line contains the project number and Country. If so, this email should be automatically copied to the respective project in Salesforce.{quote}


We need to create a cron / trigger that will check new emails, and if the email has a project id{color:#ff5630} (ex: ROKU-123) {color}then it should be associated with the project in salesforce. In salesforce the UI under the “EMails” tab should show all emails that have been recieved with the cooresponding ID in the subject

column1: Familiarization with the Orbis-Site-Portal Project Frontend
column28: Review and study the frontend of the Orbis-Site-Portal project to understand its structure, functionality, and underlying technologies, with the goal of contributing effectively to future tasks.
column109:
29/Oct/25 6:17 AM;712020:8bed8d8d-8a55-42b7-b3c8-8062e9fcedef;h3. A complete review of the orbis-site-portal project was conducted to understand its structure, dependencies, and main functionalities.

h4. The following main folders were explored:

* src
* app (application core)
* components (application components)
* data (fixed data)
* hooks (reusable component logic)
* lib (configuration)
* services (API communication)
* stores (global state)
* types (types and interfaces)
* utils (auxiliary functions)

h4. The dependencies in package.json were reviewed:

* directus (CMS)
* zod (data validation)
* zustand (global state management)
* husky (pre-commit scripts)
* cookies-next (cookie handling)
* framer-motion (React animations)
* momento (date handling)
* openai (Openai API)
* pdf-parse (reading and extracting text from PDFs)
* three (graphics and visualization)
* topojson-client (maps)

h4. The main system flow was analyzed:

app->company = localhost:3000/company
app->contact = localhost:3000/contact
app->dashboard = localhost:3000/dashboard (requires login)
app->login = localhost:3000/login
app->logout = localhost:3000/logout
app->register = localhost:3000/register
app->regulatory-updates = localhost:3000/news (requires login)
app->request-quote = 'No existing route'
app->services = localhost:3000/services
app->whitepapers = 'No existing route'
app->wiki = localhost:3000/wiki (not found)
app->api = mini backend with functions for:

* authentication via DirectUS
* retrieving countries
* functions of frequency-table
* quote functions
* conversations with AI agent

h4. The project was run locally.

!page_running.png|width=617,alt="page_running.png"!

!login.png|width=617,alt="login.png"!

!contact_bloqued_zscaler.png|width=617,alt="contact_bloqued_zscaler.png"!

h4. 
Notes:

* There are unused files and components.
* Pages should be separated into components.
* I was unable to authenticate via DirectUS to verify functionality. At this point, I only reviewed the logic through the code, without testing it with the application.
* Some external components are blocked by zscaler

column1: Automatic assignment of Reviewers and Quoters
column28:
When a new quote is created from the website or from OIS, the system must automatically assign reviewers and quoters, *without* using the logic based on the *Workteams* module.

Automatic assignment will be based on the *historical frequency* with *which* a *user* has been *assigned* to a specific account and contact.

For example, if *Gabriel* has been assigned to the *Sony* account 5 times and Alan 3 times, Gabriel will be selected as the default quoter. The same criteria apply to reviewers.

If there is a tie between users, then assign all tied users.  
If no user is found for an account, then Gabriel is assigned by default as the Quoter, and leave the Reviewer blank. The Quoter cannot be left blank.

AC:

* -Refactor the code to eliminate dependency on the Workteams module.-
* -Identify the user most frequently assigned as a reviewer and quoter for each account/contact.-
* -Automatically assign that user as a reviewer or quoter when creating the quote.-
* -The picklist of reviewers and quoters must continue to show the users available in Salesforce.-
* -Users in Sales or Finance must be able to manually modify automatically assigned reviewers and quoters.-

column118: Duplicated models issue in Salesforce quotes

column1: Duplicated models issue in Salesforce quotes
column28:
h3. Description:

During the OIS quoting process, at the *APROVED* stage, quote lines are sent again to Salesforce. However, since the models don’t include the *"Model Salesforce ID"*, Salesforce treats them as new and recreates them, causing *duplicated models*.

As a result, the *translation API* updates the latest (duplicated) model, while the Finance quote is still linked to the original model (which remains untranslated).

h3. Solution:

At the *COMPLETED* stage, Salesforce should return the generated *product and model IDs* to OIS. OIS should store these IDs, so that at the *APROVED* stage, existing models are updated instead of duplicated.

column109:
29/Sep/25 11:36 AM;712020:56472b32-05f9-4cf4-8890-dfc424324468;[~accountid:712020:8a268cf9-d226-4616-8671-a4e74428371a] 

*Summary*:

*Problem*: In the Engineering quotation, some prices that included two services were being displayed incorrectly. Even though the *QT* was 1 and the *Cost* was 7,080, the "*Sub Tota*l" appeared as 14,160.

*Investigation*: After reviewing the flow with Elvys, we found that the issue was only related to the data rendering on the UI. The visual component was using the number of services instead of the service's *QT*. This was purely a display issue, as the formulas themselves were using the correct *QT*.

*Solution*: This issue had been previously identified and corrected, and the fix was deployed to production. We verified the solution by reviewing historical quotations, and the information is now displayed correctly.

column1: Alert when opporitunity name is too long
column28: We need to alert the user when the name of an opporitunity or project name is too long. If the user is editing a name, an alert needs to show telling the user to choose a shorter name
column109:
25/Sep/25 2:05 PM;5f2ddfb0c1b9f4001cdc43c3;!Screenshot 2025-09-25 at 4.45.02 PM.png|width=1272,height=732,alt="Screenshot 2025-09-25 at 4.45.02 PM.png"!

I’m sharing a screenshot of the alert message when the opportunity name is longer than 70 characters to create the projects (PMM and FINANCE).

This alert, composed of an icon in the field and a message in the header, requires no code; it is created with Rulesets and filters.

Fulfilling the purpose of the ticket, these alerts are proposed so Monica can be alerted to this situation.

The ideal scenario would have been to create a Validation Rule for the field, but this would have led to more changes and meetings about how to shorten the names of the objects involved (Quote Engineering, Opportunities, and Quotes). As well as optimizing the name for many objects, the company often fills the name with redundant content already present in other fields, resulting in an overly long and unclear name.

It's important to remember that the Opportunity Name field is not customizable and has a capacity of 120 characters; the Project Name field is not customizable and has a capacity of 80 characters. When closed won is done and projects are created (PMM and FINANCE), the project name is: the opportunity name + ('-PMM' | '-FINANCE').

Please confirm to proceed.

column1: Feedback Adjustments / Add Payment Terms
column28:
!Screenshot 2025-08-28 151155.png|width=1557,height=777,alt="Current Work in Progress "!

^ Current work in progress

* Currency
** Should be set either as Account Profile → Currency as default selected option, if that is not set then default is USD
*** This new feature is designed to _override_ this logic on a case by case basis, but should not have to be set each time if the correct account setting exists for the client
** Currency types are USD / MXN / BRL – using the international standard label. Asia may not work as you currently have it, as there are many countries in Asia such as JPY, CNY etc which have varying exchange rates
** !image-20250828-205211.png|width=1363,height=293,alt="image-20250828-205211.png"!
Currency exchange rates are NOT EDITABLE by end user, only finance should be able to set an exchange rate. End user should only be able to _override_ the default currency on a case by case basis
* Template
** Change drop down label to be “Language”
** Should be set as Account Profile → Language as default selected option, if that is not set the English is default
*** This new feature is designed to _override_ this logic on a case by case basis, but should not have to be set each time if the correct account setting exists for the client
** Language options should only be “English” “Spanish” “Portuguese”
** Payment Terms are NOT set here. We must support all payment term options (50/50, 80/20, 0/100, 100/0, new Monthly) for each chosen language / currency. 
* Payment Terms
** Since this is closly related to language feature, we should add this at this time.
** Default should follow the outlined algo in the image

!image-20250828-214059.png|width=738,height=612,alt="image-20250828-214059.png"!

** Verify / correct logic 
*** if ANY line item is “In country testing” the quote payment term default selection is 80/20
** Dropdown added to override default labeled “Payment Term”
** Need to add a new payment term “Monthly”
*** “ORBIS to invoice monthly based on work completed during the prior month.”
* General
** Options should not exist in a mandatory pop-up. There should be a “Quote Options” button (or something like it) that triggers the view to set these options on the quote view
** Goal is to have sensible defaults that are populated from the Account Profile / Payment terms algo, with the option to override some or all 3

column1: Discover root cause of issue
Ensure last version of quote is in Salesforce
Quote Not SYNC
column28:
We have this quote:
Q-03824

[https://orbiscompliance.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Quote/0Q0RQ000002A0Tp0AK/view|https://orbiscompliance.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Quote/0Q0RQ000002A0Tp0AK/view]

It’s a quote that went through several revisions, and apparently, the latest approved version is not the one synchronized in SF. In fact, the version generated in the latest PDF does not correspond to the last time it was approved—it corresponds to an earlier version from a few days before.



*AC*

# Confirm what was indicated by Sales (quote not synchronized)
# Find the root cause of the problem and provide a solution
column109:
29/Aug/25 12:12 PM;712020:56472b32-05f9-4cf4-8890-dfc424324468;*Report*

*Detected Issue*
Upon reviewing the quote in detail, it was identified that the issue originated from a change in the flow after the deployment of the "OIS to Salesforce Synchronization" feature.

*Old Flow*

Completed (OIS)

Approved (OIS)

Salesforce → The quote was dumped and the following were created:

Engineering Quote

Opportunity

Finance Quote

*New Flow (with synchronization enabled)*

Create (OIS)
---Worker → Sync SF (create).

Completed (OIS)
------Worker → Sync SF (create or update: Engineering Quote, Opportunity, and Finance Quote).

Approved (OIS) → Direct request to Salesforce:

If a previous quote in Completed status already exists, its status is updated.

If not, all records are created in Salesforce.

*Specific Case*

Before the deployment, quote Q-03824 already existed in Salesforce in ON REVISION status with 7 items.

Later, the quote was reopened, corrected, and 1 more item was added.

This quote was moved to Completed status before the deployment.

Then, with the synchronization feature already deployed, it was changed to Approved.

Due to the logic of the new flow, in this case, the eighth item was not updated in Salesforce.

*Solution*
To resolve this issue, it was defined that in the Approved status, not only should the status be updated in Salesforce, but also a full update of the quote (including items) must be ensured.
A ticket has already been created to adjust the Approved process with this logic.

Design and develop the *user interface* and *backend* to enable the *uploading and management of PDF documents* corresponding to signed letters. The module must integrate with existing APIs and store the files in *AWS S3*, associating them with one or more selected projects.

!image-20251110-205425.png|width=392,alt="image-20251110-205425.png"!

*Acceptance criteria (AC):*

·         *Consume* the API that lists available projects.

·         *List templates* with filter option and organization by *region*.

·         *Upload PDF files* of signed letters.

o    The files must be stored on *AWS S3* (coordinate with *Kelly* and *Saul*).

o    Letters must *be registered per project*: if 3 projects are selected, 3 records with the same letter must be inserted, one for each project.

·         *List all the charges* corresponding to the selected projects.

·         Add a *button to view* the document.

·         Add a *button to remove* a record from the list.

o    Only the *user who uploaded* the file (besides the *administrator*) can delete it.

column1: API development to obtain project details (Salesforce)
[L2] Developing an API to obtain Salesforce projects
column28:
*Description:* 
Develop an endpoint that retrieves a list of projects associated with a purchase order user in Salesforce. The API should return only projects filtered by type (e.g., active or closed) and be integrated with the corresponding _bridge_.

*Endpoint:* 
GET /oc_users /:oc _email/projects /:type _project

*Parameters:*

* oc_email : OC user email.
* type_project : Type of project to query ( active , closed , etc.).

*Response (List):*

* project_id
* project_name
* country
* iso_code

*Acceptance criteria (AC):*

·         Develop the *GET API* to retrieve projects from Salesforce.

·         Integrate the API with the existing *bridge*.

·         *Document* the operation of the endpoint in *Confluence*.

02/Oct/25 5:57 AM;712020:1221cfec-034b-400d-9d4f-3d10e690f80a;User Story (Defined with Luimar Montalvo).


*User Story (high level):*

*As a* Project Engineer,
*I want to* have a well-detailed and classified repository of letters for each project,
*so* that I can efficiently select, customize, and generate letters according to the type of project (Telecom, Safety, etc.), and if necessary, add new letter templates.

 

*Tasks before implementation:*

Collect existing letter templates

* Obtain all letter templates from engineers (Luimar, Javier, etc.)
* Verify current versions and collect feedback on their usefulness and applicability.

Classify letter templates

* Classify letters according to project type (Safety, Telecom, Brazil, Mexico, Multicountry, NOM-208, etc.)
* Create a category system that allows for easy navigation and filtering in the repository.

Develop a prototype

* Design a functional prototype
* Review and approve the prototype: Present the prototype to the licensee and engineers for review, receive feedback, and obtain final approval

h3. *Main Flow*

# *The user opens a project.*
# *The system displays the list of required documents*, each with two available actions: *Approve* or *Observe*.
# *If the user selects “Approve”:*
#* The system displays an interface to *upload a file* and optionally *add a comment*.
#* The user clicks the *“Approve”* button.
#* The system *saves the information* in the {{DocumentProjectRequired}} table, *uploads the file to the S3 server*, updates the webpage via API, and *reloads the document list*.
# *If the user selects “Observe”:*
#* The system displays a window for the user to *enter a comment*.
#* The user clicks the *“Observe”* button.
#* The system *saves the information* in the {{DocumentProjectRequired}} table,  updates the webpage via API, *reloads the document list*.

----

h3. *Technical Note*

Coordinate with *Kelly* to confirm whether *Directus* can store the file in *S3* and return the corresponding *URL*.
*Salesforce* should send the following data:

* The file (in *base64* format) or null
* The *document name (ID)*
* The *project ID*
* The *status*
* Any *comments*

column1: Create Region mapping
Quote PDF Adjustments
column28:
The Quote PDF currently shows a heading and subtotal for every country in the quote. This creates alot of noise and maakes a quote many pages long when there are multiple countries.

We need to remove the heading and subtotal from each country.

Countries are also listed in a somewhat random order. 

For a given product, we need to group countries by Region (Carribean, LATAM, EU etc) with a single heading and single subtotal for the _Region_, not for each country. Each line item should still have its own individual price displayed still

grouping should be as follows:

* product / model
** region
*** item (bolivia)
*** item (brazil)
*** item (etc)

A “Grand total” should be displayed at the bottom of the quote, adding each regions subtotal together

If a region does not have any countries with a price, then that region _should not appear_ in the quote PDF.

column1: When a quote is in the “Sent to Client” status and the “Reopen” action is performed, the reviewer approval date is not updated.
column28:
Reported by Marcelo:

{quote}{quote}

|*Q-03847*|Sporton Smart Glasses, Mexico 2025|

The problem is that it was completed again by engineering, but the reviewer, Miguel, is not authorized to review it again and the previous review was left as approved without the option to approve it again with these changes made to the quote.


{quote}Miguel is listed as a reviewer, the error is that it still appears as if he already approved the quote in a previous instance. What we need for the future and in this opportunity are that every time a quote is reopened, the reviewer is also enabled as a reviewer again and does not see his past approval.{quote}

Solution:
When a quote is in the “Sent to Client” status and the “Reopen” action is performed, the reviewer approval date is updated.

column1: Implement Automatic Notification System for Quote Reopening Reviews
column28:
Currently, when a quote is reopened and requires review, there is no automatic notification system to alert the assigned reviewer (Gabriel in this case). The process relies on manual communication through chat, which creates traceability issues and potential delays in the review process.

*Problem Statement:*

* When quotes are reopened, reviewers are not automatically notified
* Manual notification through chat is the only current method
* This creates poor traceability and potential for missed reviews
* Risk of quotes sitting unreviewed due to lack of awareness

*Acceptance Criteria:*

* [ ] System automatically detects when a quote is reopened
* [ ] Automatic email notification is sent to the assigned reviewer
* [ ] Notification includes relevant quote details and reason for reopening
* [ ] Audit trail is maintained for all notifications sent
* [ ] Reviewer can acknowledge receipt of notification

column1: Adjust recipients for quote approved email
column28:
From Wayne:

 

issue is that when a quote is approved by me, the approval email should only be sent out to the Opportunity Owner.  Finance does not want to get an email for every one of Vanessa's quotes, for example...there is actually no action we would take (it is up to Vanessa to manage her Opportunity and she will alert Finance when the PO comes in)......similarly a quote owned by ORBIS COMPLIANCE LLC should not send an approval to Vanessa.

 

The idea here is that we do not want to make extraneous extra work for people....because whenever one of these emails comes in, we actually have to click on the link everytime to figure out who the Opportunity owner is, and if it isn't Finance, we just delete it

AC:
- the approval email should only be sent out to the Opportunity Owner.
- Remove any other recipients from the email (developers, etc)

column109:
19/Aug/25 5:54 AM;627bd786694943006f3538bf;*CASO DE USO:* Mostrar Detalles de Líneas de Cotización

Este caso de uso permite al usuario visualizar todos los servicios que componen una línea de cotización.

*INTERFAZ:* Cotización de Finanzas

*USUARIOS:* Personal de Finanzas y Ventas

*FLUJO NORMAL:*

Al acceder a la interfaz de cotización, el sistema muestra al usuario una lista de líneas de cotización (ITEMS). Cada línea representa un servicio principal, aunque algunas pueden contener múltiples servicios asociados. En estos casos, el sistema muestra únicamente el servicio principal de la línea. Si una línea de cotización contiene sub-servicios adicionales, las columnas *Markup , Profit Margin  y Profit Margin* indican esta situación mediante el texto "n sub-servicios", donde n representa la cantidad de sub-servicios ocultos. El usuario puede hacer clic en el botón de expandir asociado a la línea de cotización de interés. Al hacerlo, el sistema expande la línea seleccionada y muestra los sub-servicios correspondientes. Cada sub-servicio desplegado se presenta con sus respectivos detalles en las columnas *Markup , Profit Margin  y Profit Margin.*

*FLUJO ALTERNATIVO:*

En los casos en que una línea de cotización no contiene sub-servicios, el sistema muestra las columnas *Markup , Profit Margin  y Profit Margin* con valores normales, sin ningún texto adicional ni opción de expansión. Estas líneas presentan únicamente la información correspondiente al servicio principal, sin variaciones en la interfaz.

column1: Close Opportunity (Close Won/Change Status)
column28:
A button is needed that allows me to close an opportunity by entering a description and selecting the final status. This should update the opportunity in both the current system and OIS via its API.

AC:

# “Close Opportunity” button:
## Visible on the opportunity details page.
## When clicked, it opens a popup/modal with the required fields.
# Popup content:
## Text field: To enter the reason for closure (required).

Status picklist: With options:
Accept to Client (Close Won).
Declined to Client (changes status according to rules).
Declined to Finances (changes status according to rules).
.......

## “Accept” (submits data) and “Cancel” (closes popup without changes) buttons.

# Integration with OIS:
## OIS API is used to update the status.
# Editing restriction:
# When editing an opportunity, the Status field must be disabled (it can only be modified through this flow).

column1: Enable Currency / Langauge Selection for Final PDF
column28:
Vanessa had two quotes for Garmin that required Spanish language and prices set in Pesos.

This functionality currently does not exist in the new system and is a blocker for several client quotes.

Current solution is to do it manually which is error prone and very slow.

Task:

* Add an option for selecting a currency for prices to be displayed in the quote. Prices are still calculated in USD, but converted when added to the final quote

* Add an option for selecting a language for the final quote. Each language that is supported will need to have a cooresponding template

Acceptance Criteria:


* -Support the following currencies: USD, MXN, BRL-
* -Support the following languages: English, Spanish, Portuguese-
* -Abilty to switch between them (English / MXN, Spanish USD, English USD, Spanish MXN)-
* -Ability to set currency conversion formula by admin in OIS system (changes frequently) - or pulled from an external authority (salesforce?) -- needs grooming-
* -Select box for each (Currency, Lanaguage)-

* For dynamic content (comments not defined in pricelist / templates) use o3mini (azure) to translate the content for review in salesforce and final inclusio
